title: Where do evacuees go? Understanding evacuation destinations in Georgia during Hurricane Milton
content: This study proposes a methodological framework to analyze spatial and temporal patterns of Florida residents' evacuation to Georgia during Hurricane Milton, focusing on changes in Points-of-Interest (POIs) visitor share within Georgia census tracts.
snippet: This study proposes a methodological framework to analyze spatial and temporal patterns of Florida residents' evacuation to Georgia during Hurricane Milton, focusing on changes in Points-of-Interest (

title: A new multidimensional framework for risk assessment in multi-hazard context: the Hazards-Impacts matrix
content: This paper introduces a new indicator-based framework for evaluating risk in multi-hazard contexts, referred to as the “Hazards-Impacts Matrix.”
snippet: This paper introduces a new indicator-based framework for evaluating risk in multi-hazard contexts, referred to as the “Hazards-Impacts Matrix.”

title: Influence of modeling assumptions on pedestrian evacuation success for non-eruptive lahar hazards at Mount Rainier, Washington
content: This case study examines the role of time in lahar evacuations by quantifying population exposure and evacuation potential for non-eruptive lahar hazards associated with Mount Rainier, Washington.
snippet: This case study examines the role of time in lahar evacuations by quantifying population exposure and evacuation potential for non-eruptive lahar hazards associated with Mount Rainier, Washington.

title: Managing the Sundhnúkagígar–Grindavík volcanic emergency in Iceland, 2023–2026
content: This paper examines the evolving volcanic crisis on Iceland’s Reykjanes Peninsula and its implications for disaster risk reduction, infrastructure protection, and long-term displacement.
snippet: This paper examines the evolving volcanic crisis on Iceland’s Reykjanes Peninsula and its implications for disaster risk reduction, infrastructure protection, and long-term displacement.

title: “Drowning in trauma”: mental health impacts of floods in overpopulated informal settlements in South Africa
content: This study assessed the impacts of recurring floods on the mental health of Setswetla informal settlement residents in Johannesburg, South Africa and a total of 100 adult residents participated in the study.
snippet: This study assessed the impacts of recurring floods on the mental health of Setswetla informal settlement residents in Johannesburg, South Africa and a total of 100 adult residents participated in the

title: The 18th Aceh International Workshop and Expo on Sustainable Disaster Recovery
content: The 18th Aceh International Workshop and Expo on Sustainable Disaster Recovery (AIWEST-DR 2026) is bringing together researchers, practitioners, and innovators to share ideas, real solutions, and future-ready strategies for sustainable recovery.

title: Somalia CPS Webinar Series: Building Resilience Through Integrated Climate, Peace and Security Approaches
content: The Somalia Climate, Peace and Security (CPS) series serves as a strategic platform to increase momentum around the climate dialogue in Somalia.
snippet: The Somalia Climate, Peace and Security (CPS) series serves as a strategic platform to increase momentum around the climate dialogue in Somalia.

title: COPE Heatwaves
content: Travel to the city of Paris to discover how the city implements their ‘climate action plan’ to help deal with heatwaves.
snippet: Travel to the city of Paris to discover how the city implements their ‘climate action plan’ to help deal with heatwaves.

title: COPE Floods
content: Join COPE on their Floods mission to Kelantan State, Malaysia, where they put their disaster risk reduction skills into action.
snippet: Join COPE on their Floods mission to Kelantan State, Malaysia, where they put their disaster risk reduction skills into action.

title: COPE Climate Change
content: Join the COPE Squad on their journey to the United Nations Climate Conference, where they meet climate scientists and climate champions to explore key environmental challenges, and discover the power of everyday actions.
snippet: Join the COPE Squad on their journey to the United Nations Climate Conference, where they meet climate scientists and climate champions to explore key environmental challenges, and discover the power

title: In Nigeria’s Maiduguri, a doctor has a simple cure for adapting to extreme heat
content: Deaths from higher temperatures  are predicted in countries across Africa, in some cases exceeding deaths from strokes, a leading cause of mortality.
snippet: Deaths from higher temperatures are predicted in countries across Africa, in some cases exceeding deaths from strokes, a leading cause of mortality.

title: Global climate risk analysis course at Frankfurt School of Finance & Management
content: The course is designed for technical practitioners such as climate and disaster risk modelers, data analysts, researchers, and technical staff from government agencies, financial institutions, academia, and development organizations.
snippet: The course is designed for technical practitioners such as climate and disaster risk modelers, data analysts, researchers, and technical staff from government agencies, financial institutions, academi

title: The 18th Aceh International workshop and expo on sustainable disaster recovery (Hybrid)
content: The 18th Aceh International Workshop and Expo on Sustainable Disaster Recovery (AIWEST-DR 2026) is a hybrid conference, welcoming both in-person and virtual participants from around the world.
snippet: The 18th Aceh International Workshop and Expo on Sustainable Disaster Recovery (AIWEST-DR 2026) is a hybrid conference, welcoming both in-person and virtual participants from around the world.

title: Global urban exposure near volcanoes is increasing: a spatio-temporal analysis from 1975 to 2030
content: This paper is about the rapid growth of urban populations near active volcanoes and the resulting increase in exposure to volcanic hazards worldwide
snippet: This paper is about the rapid growth of urban populations near active volcanoes and the resulting increase in exposure to volcanic hazards worldwide

title: Disaster preparedness among residents of flood-prone areas in Sub-Saharan Africa: a systematic review
content: This systematic review reveals low levels of flood preparedness among residents in flood-prone areas, identifying key socioeconomic determinants and prevalent strategies, underscoring the need for targeted interventions to enhance community resilience.
snippet: This systematic review reveals low levels of flood preparedness among residents in flood-prone areas, identifying key socioeconomic determinants and prevalent strategies, underscoring the need for tar

title: Why the phrase “Super El Niño” makes Australian climate scientists roll their eyes
content: Frightening headlines predicting a Super El Niño or even a Godzilla El Niño amp up anxiety levels for farmers and residents of bushfire-prone regions.
snippet: Frightening headlines predicting a Super El Niño or even a Godzilla El Niño amp up anxiety levels for farmers and residents of bushfire-prone regions.

title: How AI’s language barrier limits climate disaster responses
content: This paper is about how AI language limitations can hinder effective climate disaster response by misinterpreting local expressions and urgency in affected communities
snippet: This paper is about how AI language limitations can hinder effective climate disaster response by misinterpreting local expressions and urgency in affected communities

title: Global forum on environmental and water resources engineering
content: The Global Forum Environmental and Water Resources Engineering (ENVIWATER2026) will bring together leading researchers, academicians, industry experts, policymakers, and sustainability practitioners from around the world.
snippet: The Global Forum Environmental and Water Resources Engineering (ENVIWATER2026) will bring together leading researchers, academicians, industry experts, policymakers, and sustainability practitioners f

title: Toolkit for applying a gender-transformative approach to women-led climate resilience
content: Practical toolkit guiding gender-transformative approaches to strengthen women’s leadership in climate resilience and disaster risk reduction.
snippet: Practical toolkit guiding gender-transformative approaches to strengthen women’s leadership in climate resilience and disaster risk reduction.

title: Discover Public Health (Springer)
content: Discover Public Health is a fully open access, peer-reviewed journal that supports multidisciplinary research and policy developments across all fields relevant to public health and societal impacts related to this.
snippet: Discover Public Health is a fully open access, peer-reviewed journal that supports multidisciplinary research and policy developments across all fields relevant to public health and societal impacts r

title: Readying health facilities for climate-driven emergencies
content: This report examines how strengthening health facilities can enhance disaster risk reduction in the context of climate-driven emergencies, with a focus on safeguarding women’s health.
snippet: This report examines how strengthening health facilities can enhance disaster risk reduction in the context of climate-driven emergencies, with a focus on safeguarding women’s health.

title: Pathfinder.org
content: For nearly 70 years, Pathfinder International has been a catalyst for systems transformation, inclusive growth, and resilient communities in over 120 countries across the world.
snippet: For nearly 70 years, Pathfinder International has been a catalyst for systems transformation, inclusive growth, and resilient communities in over 120 countries across the world.

title: Resilience, adaptation, and inclusion in Nepal (RAIN) programme GEDSI analysis report, 2025
content: This analysis observes how existing gender and social inequalities can impact the project activities and how projects can impact the gender dynamics of the work site.
snippet: This analysis observes how existing gender and social inequalities can impact the project activities and how projects can impact the gender dynamics of the work site.

title: Vulnerability of people in wood-frame buildings to rapid flow-type landslides
content: The authors developed an empirical life-loss vulnerability curve for people inside wood-frame buildings as a function of impact pressures from flow-type landslides.
snippet: The authors developed an empirical life-loss vulnerability curve for people inside wood-frame buildings as a function of impact pressures from flow-type landslides.

title: Hazard mapping of hydrological disasters in the municipality of Porto Alegre/RS
content: This study proposes a hydrological hazard map for Porto Alegre as a reliable and accessible tool to support government planning and risk management.
snippet: This study proposes a hydrological hazard map for Porto Alegre as a reliable and accessible tool to support government planning and risk management.

title: From local voices to global action: Women leaders driving climate and disaster resilience
content: Policy dialogue highlighting women leaders and grassroots groups driving climate and disaster resilience in Europe and Central Asia, fostering partnerships to advance disaster risk reduction.
snippet: Policy dialogue highlighting women leaders and grassroots groups driving climate and disaster resilience in Europe and Central Asia, fostering partnerships to advance disaster risk reduction.

title: Evaluating the disaster risk model of Myanmar: from index to negative binomial regression
content: This study evaluates the predictive efficacy of conventional disaster risk index (DRI) modelling against empirical generalised linear model (GLM) analysis using data from 71 districts in Myanmar.
snippet: This study evaluates the predictive efficacy of conventional disaster risk index (DRI) modelling against empirical generalised linear model (GLM) analysis using data from 71 districts in Myanmar.

title: A geostatistical imputation of first floor elevation data for mapping flood vulnerability
content: To facilitate efficient First Floor Elevation (FFE) data collection, this paper explores the extent to which FFEs for an entire community’s building stock can be estimated using limited FFE data records.
snippet: To facilitate efficient First Floor Elevation (FFE) data collection, this paper explores the extent to which FFEs for an entire community’s building stock can be estimated using limited FFE data recor

title: Static maps, dynamic threats: re-evaluating U.S. wildfire risk with spatiotemporal clustering
content: This study provides a national-scale comparison of purely spatial and spatiotemporal clustering methods to identify wildfire (WF) hotspots across the contiguous United States (CONUS) from 2014 to 2024.
snippet: This study provides a national-scale comparison of purely spatial and spatiotemporal clustering methods to identify wildfire (WF) hotspots across the contiguous United States (CONUS) from 2014 to 2024

title: Responding to floods in Pakistan with adaptive social protection: How to keep an inundated country afloat
content: This note aims to identify ways to work with the existing social protection system to build a well-functioning Adaptive Social Protection System, which would build pre-shock resilience and provide rapid and adequate response in times of crises.
snippet: This note aims to identify ways to work with the existing social protection system to build a well-functioning Adaptive Social Protection System, which would build pre-shock resilience and provide rap

title: Ending gender-based violence in disaster risks and climate resilience by 2030
content: This issue of Southasiadisasters.net examines how disasters, displacement, extreme heat, migration, water scarcity, recovery processes, and climate adaptation can intensify violence against women and girls when protection systems are weak or absent.
snippet: This issue of Southasiadisasters.net examines how disasters, displacement, extreme heat, migration, water scarcity, recovery processes, and climate adaptation can intensify violence against women and

title: UNFCCC call for input from ISC members – mapping progress in filling climate change research gaps
content: Since 2005 scientists & policymakers have identified important gaps in climate research in their deliberations under the SBSTA. The UNFCCC has called for input from ISC Members and the scientific community to highlight progress made in filling these gaps
snippet: Since 2005 scientists & policymakers have identified important gaps in climate research in their deliberations under the SBSTA. The UNFCCC has called for input from ISC Members and the scientific

title: Sinking land drives coastal flood risk on densely populated Java Island
content: A study published in Science Advances reveals that sinking land — not just rising oceans alone — will be the main cause of future coastal flooding along Indonesia’s densely populated Java Island, putting millions at risk sooner than expected.
snippet: A study published in Science Advances reveals that sinking land — not just rising oceans alone — will be the main cause of future coastal flooding along Indonesia’s densely populated Java Island, putt

title: Confronting climate migration in drought-hit East Africa
content: Local solutions are needed to address migration and boost resilience, agreed researchers and policymakers at a conference in Ethiopia
snippet: Local solutions are needed to address migration and boost resilience, agreed researchers and policymakers at a conference in Ethiopia

title: Landscape of climate finance in Ethiopia
content: Ethiopia’s climate policy framework is increasingly investment-oriented, moving from ambition to action.
snippet: Ethiopia’s climate policy framework is increasingly investment-oriented, moving from ambition to action.

title: From forecast to action: using flood impact storylines to optimize mobile levee deployment and emergency response strategies
content: This study shows how flood impact storylines can help emergency managers decide where and when to deploy mobile levees, reducing damage and buying critical time for evacuation during extreme flood events.
snippet: This study shows how flood impact storylines can help emergency managers decide where and when to deploy mobile levees, reducing damage and buying critical time for evacuation during extreme flood eve

title: The Global Risk Modelling Alliance’s (GRMA) Call for proposals for Malawi is now open.
content: The GRMA aims to fill critical model and data gaps and enhance (sub-)sovereign climate and disaster risk assessment capabilities. This Call for Proposals (CfP) reflects the gaps and needs identified and co-defined together with country partners.

title: State consultant in Gujarat: INSPIRE India
content: The State Consultant in Gujarat will be responsible for coordinating and implementing state-level activities under the INSPIRE India program.
snippet: The State Consultant in Gujarat will be responsible for coordinating and implementing state-level activities under the INSPIRE India program.

title: Smokey knows: President Trump’s Forest service restructuring is bad news
content: The expansive restructuring of the agency is irreversibly destructive to the federal scientific enterprise and leaves the nation to face growing climate threats with fewer experts predicting and managing wildfires.
snippet: The expansive restructuring of the agency is irreversibly destructive to the federal scientific enterprise and leaves the nation to face growing climate threats with fewer experts predicting and manag

title: As Indian cities struggle to plan for heat, the most vulnerable suffer
content: Adaptation to heat from climate change, which drives temperatures higher, requires rethinking how cities are planned and developed, say experts in India and worldwide.
snippet: Adaptation to heat from climate change, which drives temperatures higher, requires rethinking how cities are planned and developed, say experts in India and worldwide.

title: Cities battle urban heat: Green solutions for a warming climate
content: UHIs develop when buildings, roads, and other infrastructure made of materials such as steel, stone, and concrete absorb heat from the sun during the day and then release it back into the atmosphere in the afternoon and evening.
snippet: UHIs develop when buildings, roads, and other infrastructure made of materials such as steel, stone, and concrete absorb heat from the sun during the day and then release it back into the atmosphere i

title: Research shows how forests can prevent floods of all sizes
content: Forests play a crucial and often underestimated role in reducing flood risk of all magnitudes, highlighting the need to integrate nature-based solutions with traditional infrastructure for more effective climate adaptation.
snippet: Forests play a crucial and often underestimated role in reducing flood risk of all magnitudes, highlighting the need to integrate nature-based solutions with traditional infrastructure for more effect

title: Preparing for climate extremes means we must work with uncertainty
content: Climate change is expected to increase the frequency and intensity of extreme weather events, which are creating significant uncertainties for local communities as well as private and public agencies across the world.
snippet: Climate change is expected to increase the frequency and intensity of extreme weather events, which are creating significant uncertainties for local communities as well as private and public agencies

title: Risk to resilience: A socio-ecological perspective on the Indians Sundarbans
content: This book examines how socio-ecological factors shape vulnerability and resilience in the Indian Sundarbans, highlighting community-based and ecosystem-based strategies to address multi-hazard risks.
snippet: This book examines how socio-ecological factors shape vulnerability and resilience in the Indian Sundarbans, highlighting community-based and ecosystem-based strategies to address multi-hazard risks.

title: Public financial management for disaster response and adaptive social protection in the Sahel
content: This report examines how governments across Burkina Faso, Chad, Mali, Mauritania and Senegal can better plan and budget for disasters before they strike, setting out practical recommendations for building more responsive social protection systems.
